title: Release Automation, npm Trusted Publishing, and Change Control · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Release Approval Separation Duty
summary: A release is triggered from an unreviewed tag, rebuilds after approval, publishes npm with a long-lived token, mixes changelog generation with mutation, and cannot prove package or image provenance. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci release approval separation duty increment in multi-artifact release and publication policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.
Verify package contents, pack list, version, repository identity, source commit, workflow, transparency entry, signatures, and provenance before adoption.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk. A defensible solution applies Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-artifact release and publication policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-release-approval-separation-duty — Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-cd-releases-packages-change-control-2
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-releases-packages-change-control-2
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: worked cd releases packages change control 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      source: protected-reviewed-tag
      checks: required-at-source-sha
      build: once
      approval: promote-existing-artifact
      image: immutable-digest
      npm: trusted-publishing
      npm-minimum: 11.5.1
      node-minimum: 22.14.0
      id-token: release-job-only
      package-provenance: verified
      separation: author-reviewer-builder-approver-publisher
    verify: compare tag authority source checks artifact digest package contents trusted publisher identity provenance approval and published result
    failure: Reject tag-only authorization, per-environment rebuild, long-lived publish token, OIDC for private install, provenance as benign-code proof, or approval count as safety.
    repair: Protect source authority, build once, approve promotion, publish through exact OIDC identity, verify package provenance, and separate duties by risk.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: More manual approval steps always improve release safety. This fails the Ci Release Approval Separation Duty contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-release-approval-separation-duty-8bodea
  - competency: ci-release-approval-separation-duty
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: release approval separation duty
    environment: public fork pull request with no repository write access
    fault-injection: cancel an older run while its external mutation is in progress
    retained-proof: Cloud Run revision readiness smoke traffic signals abort and rollback
    case-key: 8bodea
    change: |
      source: protected-reviewed-tag
      checks: required-at-source-sha
      build: once
      approval: promote-existing-artifact
      image: immutable-digest
      npm: trusted-publishing
      npm-minimum: 11.5.1
      node-minimum: 22.14.0
      id-token: release-job-only
      package-provenance: verified
      separation: author-reviewer-builder-approver-publisher
    verify: compare tag authority source checks artifact digest package contents trusted publisher identity provenance approval and published result
    failure: Reject tag-only authorization, per-environment rebuild, long-lived publish token, OIDC for private install, provenance as benign-code proof, or approval count as safety.
    repair: Protect source authority, build once, approve promotion, publish through exact OIDC identity, verify package provenance, and separate duties by risk.
